reasoning: The question asks whether there will be more than 10 times the baseline of 'Explosions/Remote violence' events in Canada in a 30-day window. The baseline calculation (one plus the 30-day average over the prior 360 days) is currently 1.000, meaning there were 0 events in the past year. Ten times this baseline is 10.0, so the threshold is 11 or more events in a 30-day window. Canada has virtually zero 'Explosions/Remote violence' incidents per month, and has not experienced a campaign of bombings or remote violence since the FLQ in the 1960s/70s. Reaching 11 events in a 30-day period would require an unprecedented sudden outbreak of organized terrorism or armed conflict. I assign the baseline floor probability of 0.02 to the near-term dates and slightly increase for later dates as per instructions to reflect increasing uncertainty.
